note: Certificate programmes in agriculture are offered at government agricultural colleges including Kwadaso, Ejura, Ohawu, Damongo, and the Animal Health and Production College, Pong.
Admissions are centralised through the Ministry of Food and Agriculture Schools portal at mofaschools.edu.gh; application vouchers are purchased at GCB Bank branches.
Certificate graduates may progress to Diploma programmes (2 years via sandwich/weekend) upon completion and at least 2 years of working experience.
All programmes are accredited by the Ghana Tertiary Education Commission (GTEC) and overseen by the Ministry of Food and Agriculture.

fees: Government Agricultural Colleges: GHC 1,200 - GHC 2,500 per year
Additional levies (SRC, Hall, Exams): approx. GHC 500 per year
Application voucher: GHC 150
cutt_off: 2025: 5 passes including English, Mathematics, Integrated Science and two electives
2024: 5 passes including English, Mathematics, Integrated Science and two electives
2023: 5 passes including English, Mathematics, Integrated Science and two electives
mode: Full-time residential programme
Classroom theory and farm practical sessions
Supervised field attachments and project work
entry_requirements: WASSCE: Minimum passes (D7) in English, Mathematics, and Integrated Science
Passes (A1-E8) in at least two elective subjects in science, agricultural science, or related areas
Five (5) passes overall including core and elective subjects
Age limit: 16-35 years; interview may be required
level_note: The Certificate in General Agriculture is the foundational agricultural qualification at Ghana's government agricultural colleges.
Certificate holders can top up to a Diploma in General Agriculture via a 2-year sandwich or weekend programme after 2 years of work experience.

fees: Government Agricultural Colleges: GHC 1,200 - GHC 2,500 per year
Animal Health and Production College, Pong: fees similar to peer government colleges
Additional levies (SRC, Hall, Exams): approx. GHC 500 per year
cutt_off: 2025: 5 passes including English, Mathematics, Integrated Science and two relevant electives
2024: 5 passes including English, Mathematics, Integrated Science and two relevant electives
2023: 5 passes including English, Mathematics, Integrated Science and two relevant electives
mode: Full-time residential programme
Theory combined with livestock practical sessions
Field attachments at veterinary clinics and farms
entry_requirements: WASSCE: Minimum passes (D7) in English, Mathematics, and Integrated Science
Passes in at least two elective subjects preferably Biology, Chemistry, or Animal Husbandry
Five (5) passes overall
Age limit: 16-35 years; passion for animal husbandry and livestock management advantageous
level_note: The Certificate in Animal Health is primarily offered at the Animal Health and Production College, Pong, in the Northern Region.
Graduates work within Ghana Health Service veterinary units, livestock farms, and the Ghana Health Service animal health surveillance network.

fees: Government and Private Agricultural Colleges: GHC 2,000 - GHC 4,500 per year
Ohawu Agricultural College (CGT Programme): contact institution for exact fee
Additional levies: approx. GHC 500 per year
cutt_off: 2025: 5 passes including English, Mathematics, Integrated Science and two electives; prior agricultural knowledge an advantage
2024: 5 passes including English, Mathematics, Integrated Science and two electives
2023: 5 passes including English, Mathematics, Integrated Science and two electives
mode: Full-time practical and hands-on programme
In-greenhouse crop production training
Entrepreneurship and farm business management sessions
entry_requirements: WASSCE: Minimum passes (D7) in English, Mathematics, and Integrated Science
Passes in at least two elective subjects; agricultural background preferred
Prior knowledge or experience in agriculture is an advantage
Programme targets aspiring agri-entrepreneurs and farm managers
level_note: The Certificate in Greenhouse Technology is a short practical programme developed in partnership with agricultural professionals, notably at Ohawu Agricultural College.
Graduates are equipped to run commercial greenhouse vegetable and flower operations, a growing sector in Ghana's urban and peri-urban farming landscape.

note: Diploma programmes are the flagship offerings of Ghana's government agricultural colleges and are fully accredited by the Ghana Tertiary Education Commission (GTEC).
The Diploma in General Agriculture is a 3-year programme for direct WASSCE/SSSCE entry; a 2-year sandwich route exists for Certificate holders with 2 years of work experience.
Key agricultural colleges offering Diploma programmes include Kwadaso (Kumasi), Ejura (Ejura), Ohawu (Volta Region), and Damongo (Savannah Region).
Diploma holders with a Final Grade Point Average (FGPA) of 3.00 or better are eligible to apply for BSc Agriculture programmes at universities such as UG and KNUST, entering at Level 200.

fees: Government Agricultural Colleges: GHC 1,500 - GHC 3,000 per year
Additional levies (SRC, Hall, Exams): approx. GHC 660 per year
Application voucher: GHC 150
cutt_off: 2025: Aggregate 24 or better (WASSCE); Credits (A1-C6) in English, Mathematics, Integrated Science and three electives
2024: Aggregate 24 or better (WASSCE)
2023: Aggregate 24 or better (WASSCE)
mode: Full-time residential programme
Lecture-based theory and farm practical work
Field attachments, project work, and industry visits
entry_requirements: WASSCE: Credits (A1-C6) in English, Mathematics, and Integrated Science
Credits (A1-C6) in three elective subjects from Physics, Chemistry, Biology, General Agriculture, Fisheries, Animal Husbandry, or Horticulture
Overall aggregate of 24 or better
Age limit: 16-35 years; interview required at most colleges
level_note: The Diploma in General Agriculture is the most widely enrolled agricultural programme in Ghana, offered at all five government agricultural colleges.
Graduates are eligible for roles within the Ghana Health Service agricultural directorates, NGOs, and can upgrade to a BSc Agriculture degree at accredited universities.

fees: Animal Health and Production College, Pong: GHC 1,500 - GHC 3,000 per year
Additional levies (SRC, Hall, Exams): approx. GHC 660 per year
Application voucher: GHC 150
cutt_off: 2025: Aggregate 24 or better (WASSCE); Credits in English, Mathematics, Integrated Science and three relevant electives
2024: Aggregate 24 or better (WASSCE)
2023: Aggregate 24 or better (WASSCE)
mode: Full-time residential programme
Veterinary clinical practicals and livestock farm rotations
Field disease surveillance and outreach attachments
entry_requirements: WASSCE: Credits (A1-C6) in English, Mathematics, and Integrated Science
Credits (A1-C6) in three elective subjects preferably Biology, Chemistry, and Animal Husbandry or General Agriculture
Overall aggregate of 24 or better
Age limit: 16-35 years; must be in good health and pass a medical examination
level_note: The Diploma in Animal Health is the primary qualification for mid-level animal health officers within the Ghana Veterinary Service and Ghana Health Service.
Graduates support national programmes in livestock disease surveillance, vaccination campaigns, and food safety inspection at slaughterhouses.

note: BSc Agriculture programmes in Ghana are offered at public universities including the University of Ghana (UG, Legon), KNUST (Kumasi), University for Development Studies (UDS), and University of Energy and Natural Resources (UENR).

The general WASSCE entry requirement for all BSc Agriculture-related degree programmes is an aggregate of 24 or better with credits in English, Mathematics, Integrated Science, and three relevant elective subjects.
Diploma holders from accredited agricultural colleges with a FGPA of 3.00 or better may be admitted to Level 200 of BSc Agriculture programmes, reducing the duration to 3 years.

All degree programmes are accredited by the Ghana Tertiary Education Commission (GTEC) and graduates are recognised for professional registration with bodies such as the Ghana Institute of Agronomists.

fees: University of Ghana (Regular): GHC 3,500 - GHC 4,500 per year
KNUST (Regular): GHC 3,000 - GHC 5,000 per year
UDS (Regular): approx. GHC 2,165 per year
Fee-paying options available at KNUST and UG at higher rates
cutt_off: 2025: Aggregate 24 or better (WASSCE); UG and KNUST competitive from Aggregate 12-18
2024: Aggregate 24 or better (WASSCE)
2023: Aggregate 24 or better (WASSCE)
mode: Full-time 4-year degree programme
Lectures, laboratory work, field trips, and farm internships
Final-year capstone project or research thesis
entry_requirements: WASSCE: Credits (A1-C6) in English, Mathematics, and Integrated Science
Credits (A1-C6) in three elective subjects from Biology, Chemistry, Physics, Elective Mathematics, General Agriculture, Geography, or Economics
Overall aggregate of 24 or better
Diploma holders from accredited agricultural colleges (FGPA 3.00+) admitted to Level 200
level_note: BSc Agriculture at UG and KNUST offers specialisations including Crop Science, Animal Science, Soil Science, Agricultural Economics, Agribusiness, and Post-Harvest Technology.
Graduates are eligible for postgraduate study (MPhil, PhD) and careers in Ghana Health Service agricultural directorates, research institutions, NGOs, and international organisations.

fees: University of Ghana (Regular): GHC 3,500 - GHC 4,500 per year
KNUST (Regular): GHC 3,000 - GHC 5,000 per year
Fee-paying options available at higher rates
cutt_off: 2025: Aggregate 24 or better (WASSCE); competitive admission at UG and KNUST
2024: Aggregate 24 or better (WASSCE)
2023: Aggregate 24 or better (WASSCE)
mode: Full-time 4-year degree programme
Case studies, farm enterprise simulations, and business projects
Industry internships and agribusiness field visits
entry_requirements: WASSCE: Credits (A1-C6) in English, Mathematics, and Integrated Science
Credits (A1-C6) in three elective subjects; Science, Business, or General Arts backgrounds accepted
Science applicants: Biology or Chemistry plus Economics or Mathematics preferred
Overall aggregate of 24 or better
level_note: BSc Agribusiness Management combines agriculture, economics, and business principles to train graduates for commercial roles across the agricultural value chain.
Offered at the University of Ghana School of Agriculture and KNUST Department of Agricultural Economics, Agribusiness and Extension.

fees: University of Ghana (Regular): GHC 3,500 - GHC 5,000 per year
KNUST (Regular): GHC 3,500 - GHC 5,500 per year
Fee-paying options available at higher rates
cutt_off: 2025: Aggregate 24 or better (WASSCE); Biology and Chemistry credits essential
2024: Aggregate 24 or better (WASSCE)
2023: Aggregate 24 or better (WASSCE)
mode: Full-time 4-year science-intensive programme
Molecular laboratory practicals and bioinformatics training
Research projects and attachments at CSIR and biotechnology institutes
entry_requirements: WASSCE: Credits (A1-C6) in English, Mathematics, and Integrated Science
Credits (A1-C6) in Biology, Chemistry, and one of Physics, Elective Mathematics, or General Agriculture
Overall aggregate of 24 or better
Strong science background required; Biology and Chemistry are mandatory electives
level_note: BSc Agricultural Biotechnology is offered at the University of Ghana (Legon) and KNUST, both of which have dedicated biotechnology laboratories and research facilities.
Graduates support Ghana's crop improvement programmes, including work on drought-resistant and pest-resistant varieties through institutions such as CSIR-SARI and CSIR-PGRRI.

Overall aggregate of 24 or better
Interest in animal-based food science and technology advantageous
level_note: BSc Dairy and Meat Science and Technology is a niche but growing programme at KNUST and UG, addressing Ghana's expanding livestock and food processing sectors.
Graduates are well-positioned for careers in Ghana's rapidly growing dairy and meat value chains, as well as in food safety regulation and export compliance.

fees: University of Ghana (Regular): GHC 3,500 - GHC 5,000 per year

level_note: BSc Landscape Design and Management is offered at KNUST's Faculty of Biosciences and at the University of Ghana School of Agriculture.
The programme addresses Ghana's growing demand for professional landscape architects and urban green space managers in real estate and municipal development.

note: MPhil and PhD Agriculture programmes are offered at the University of Ghana (Legon), KNUST (Kumasi), and the University for Development Studies (UDS).
MPhil programmes typically run for 2 years (coursework and research) while PhD programmes run for 3-4 years and require an MPhil or equivalent research degree for entry.

Specialisations available at the MPhil and PhD level include Crop Science, Animal Science, Soil Science, Agribusiness, Post-Harvest Technology, Biotechnology, Agricultural Economics, Agroforestry, and more.
KNUST MPhil Agriculture tuition fees are approximately GHC 4,773 per year plus additional charges of GHC 3,280, totalling approximately GHC 8,000 - GHC 10,000 per year.

career: FAO and World Bank Agricultural Advisor

fees: KNUST MPhil Agriculture: approx. GHC 4,773 tuition + GHC 3,487 other charges per year
University of Ghana MPhil Agriculture: approx. GHC 5,000 - GHC 7,500 per year
UDS MPhil Agriculture: approx. GHC 3,500 - GHC 5,000 per year

required: Good first degree (minimum Second Class Lower) in Agriculture or related field

mode: Full-time or part-time coursework and research programme
Thesis-based research in specialisation area
Seminars, lab work, and field research
entry_requirements: Good first degree (minimum Second Class Lower Division) in Agriculture, Biological Sciences, or a relevant field
Research proposal outlining intended area of study
Two academic or professional referees
Interview required at most institutions; GRE or equivalent may be needed for some universities
level_note: MPhil Agriculture programmes at UG and KNUST cover over 20 specialisations including Agronomy, Animal Breeding, Biotechnology, Post-Harvest Technology, and Agricultural Economics.
MPhil graduates are eligible to register for PhD programmes and pursue academic or high-level research careers locally and internationally.

fees: KNUST PhD Agriculture: approx. GHC 5,000 - GHC 8,000 per year plus research fees
University of Ghana PhD Agriculture: approx. GHC 6,000 - GHC 10,000 per year
Research and thesis fees charged separately; international students pay in USD

required: MPhil degree (with research) in Agriculture or a closely related field

mode: Full-time or part-time research programme
Original research, thesis, and seminars
International collaborations and conference presentations
entry_requirements: MPhil degree (with research component) in Agriculture or a closely related field from a recognised institution
Detailed research proposal accepted by a faculty supervisor
Two academic referees with strong research credentials
Evidence of research capacity (publications, presentations, or research experience) is an advantage
level_note: PhD Agriculture programmes at UG, KNUST, and UDS are open to specialisations spanning the full breadth of agricultural science, from plant breeding and soil science to agricultural economics and food systems.
PhD graduates are the primary pipeline for academic staff, national research institute scientists, and senior roles in international agricultural organisations operating in Ghana and Africa.

note: Short courses in agriculture are offered at government agricultural colleges including Kwadaso, Ejura, Ohawu, and Damongo, as well as at private agri-training centres.
Duration ranges from 1 to 16 weeks depending on the course; no formal WASSCE requirement applies — basic literacy and interest in the subject area are sufficient.
Short courses are designed for farmers, agri-entrepreneurs, rural youth, and professionals seeking practical skills to start or improve farm enterprises.
Certificates of completion are issued by the training institution and may be recognised for access to agricultural loans, government grants, and extension support programmes.

fees: Government Agricultural Colleges: GHC 500 - GHC 1,500 per course
Private Training Centres: GHC 800 - GHC 2,500 per course
Includes training materials and field practical sessions

required: Basic literacy in English and numeracy

additional: Interest in bee management and honey production

mode: Practical hands-on training at apiary sites
Classroom sessions on bee biology and hive management
Field demonstrations and take-home starter kits at some centres
entry_requirements: No formal WASSCE requirement
Basic literacy (reading and writing in English) required
Open to farmers, rural youth, and agri-entrepreneurs aged 18 and above
Interest in beekeeping and willingness to work with live bees required
level_note: Apiculture is a rapidly growing income-generating enterprise in Ghana, with strong demand for quality honey and beeswax in domestic and export markets.
Short course graduates can access start-up support through Planting for Export and Rural Development (PERD) and other MOFA-linked programmes.

fees: Government Agricultural Colleges: GHC 500 - GHC 1,500 per course
Private Training Centres: GHC 800 - GHC 2,500 per course

required: Basic literacy in English and numeracy

additional: Interest in livestock and poultry management

mode: Practical training on poultry farms
Classroom sessions on nutrition, disease, and biosecurity
Farm visits and enterprise planning workshops
entry_requirements: No formal WASSCE requirement
Basic literacy required
Open to farmers, youth, and agri-entrepreneurs aged 18 and above
level_note: Poultry production is one of the most accessible and high-demand agricultural enterprises in Ghana, with strong domestic markets for eggs and broiler meat.
Graduates can access MOFA's Poultry for Food and Jobs programme and ADB Bank agricultural loan schemes.

fees: Government Agricultural Colleges: GHC 500 - GHC 1,500 per course
Private Training Centres: GHC 800 - GHC 2,000 per course

required: Basic literacy in English and numeracy

additional: Interest in indoor or controlled-environment farming

mode: Hands-on indoor cultivation training
Substrate preparation and spawn inoculation practicals
Business and marketing guidance for mushroom enterprises
entry_requirements: No formal WASSCE requirement
Basic literacy required
Open to all ages 18 and above with interest in agri-enterprise
level_note: Mushroom production is a growing high-value enterprise in Ghana, requiring low land area and capital while offering fast returns.
Kwadaso Agricultural College and Ohawu Agricultural College are among the leading centres offering mushroom production training in Ghana.

fees: Government Agricultural Colleges: GHC 500 - GHC 1,500 per course
Private Training Centres: GHC 800 - GHC 2,500 per course

required: Basic literacy in English and numeracy

additional: Interest in beef or dairy cattle management

mode: Farm-based practical training with live cattle
Classroom sessions on nutrition, health, and breeding
Field visits to commercial cattle ranches
entry_requirements: No formal WASSCE requirement
Basic literacy required
Open to farmers and agri-entrepreneurs aged 18 and above
level_note: Cattle production training is particularly relevant in the savannah regions of Ghana where cattle farming is a major livelihood activity.
Graduates can access MOFA's Ruminant for Food and Jobs initiative and the Ghana Commercial Agriculture Project (GCAP) support schemes.

Basic literacy required

level_note: Sheep and goat farming is widely practised across all regions of Ghana and forms a key component of household food security and income.
Short course graduates may access financing through the Agricultural Development Bank (ADB) and MOFA's Ruminant for Food and Jobs programme.

fees: Government Agricultural Colleges: GHC 400 - GHC 1,200 per course
Private Training Centres: GHC 600 - GHC 1,800 per course

required: Basic literacy in English and numeracy

additional: Interest in small livestock or peri-urban farming

mode: Hands-on practical training with live rabbits
Classroom sessions on housing, feeding, and disease management
Business planning for rabbit enterprise start-up
entry_requirements: No formal WASSCE requirement
Basic literacy required
Open to youth, urban and peri-urban agri-entrepreneurs aged 18 and above
level_note: Rabbit farming is promoted in Ghana as a low-cost, high-protein meat enterprise suitable for urban, peri-urban, and rural settings.
Kwadaso Agricultural College includes rabbit production as part of its portfolio of short courses for agri-entrepreneurship development.

fees: Government Agricultural Colleges: GHC 400 - GHC 1,200 per course
Private Training Centres: GHC 600 - GHC 1,800 per course

required: Basic literacy in English and numeracy

mode: Hands-on practical training with live grasscutters

entry_requirements: No formal WASSCE requirement

Open to farmers, rural youth, and agri-entrepreneurs aged 18 and above
level_note: Grasscutter (Thryonomys swinderianus) farming is a high-demand, culturally accepted bushmeat alternative in Ghana, with strong urban and rural market uptake.
Kwadaso Agricultural College and Ejura Agricultural College are among the key institutions offering grasscutter production training in Ghana.
